_comment: Shared Fleet Status Contract v1 validation vectors. Consumed today by fleet_conformance.py --self-test (Python, three copies: claude-code, root .claude, copilot/scripts). Intended as the same fixture set a future fleet-contract Rust crate self-tests against (docs/grimoire/design/fleet-status-contract.md §Crate spec pointer), so the Rust and Python validators are proven to agree byte-for-byte on the same payloads rather than drifting apart with hand-copied fixtures. Each case names a JSON payload under cases/, the shape it validates against ('full' or 'minimal'), and the expected outcome ('pass' or 'fail' with a minimum error count). Cases are additive-optional: a new field or rule adds a case here rather than editing an existing one, mirroring the contract's own additive-optional evolution rule (fleet-status-contract.md §3.3).

cases.name: full-omitted-update-keys
cases.note: update.available / update.last_checked keys absent entirely — absent key ≡ null per §1.3.

cases.name: full-missing-fields
cases.note: instance/update missing; build/runtime missing required sub-fields.

cases.name: full-bad-framework-version
cases.note: empty framework_version + non-string last_synced — two distinct violations.

cases.name: minimal-with-framework-sync-fields
cases.note: framework_version/last_synced are non-sensitive and MAY appear in the minimal shape too (§1.4).

cases.name: minimal-with-forbidden-fields
cases.note: build.git_sha and top-level instance MUST be absent from the minimal shape.
